evidence_quotes.quote: prices for digital goods easily visible and also without misleading prices
It would be great to have a requirement for marketplaces to show how long sale for a good has been available or when it started.

that they actually own the bought goods from perspective of the law without having to worry manufacturer, trader, publisher or developer disabling these bought goods, digital or otherwise, license or actual media.

If application, videogame, license, tool or toy requires access to online service to function the some means need to be made available for user, consumer or business, to keep using aforementioned goods when eventually the service is brought down.

If I were a business and bought an application or license to application that my business needs to conduct its business it shan't be disabled or removed at the whim of trader, manufacturer, publisher or developer.

paragraphs.text: So this Digital Fairness Act seems to have good thing going on for it. I would very much prefer to have prices for digital goods easily visible and also without misleading prices which has been common practice especially when it comes to videogames as a service. I would also love to see that when consumer buys something digital and physical as well, that they actually own the bought goods from perspective of the law without having to worry manufacturer, trader, publisher or developer disabling these bought goods, digital or otherwise, license or actual media. When you've bought something, you should be able to believe that you own the copy you bought (of course intellectual property should not transfer unless otherwise stated). This should also impact businesses. If I were a business and bought an application or license to application that my business needs to conduct its business it shan't be disabled or removed at the whim of trader, manufacturer, publisher or developer. I should be allowed to use it without a fear until I find replacement or the need otherwise disappears. If application, videogame, license, tool or toy requires access to online service to function the some means need to be made available for user, consumer or business, to keep using aforementioned goods when eventually the service is brought down. I don't need to expect full functionality, but some means to keep things working and functional should be required in one form or other. I'd expand the objectives and policies by also requiring strong encryption and protection to make sure consumers and businesses don't have to fear for their data or PII ending at t he hands those that shouldn't have access. Also consumers and businesses should be allowed to trust that they can securely communicate with especially family, but also friends, partners, other businesses, etc. This would also protect children better than elsewhere suggested chat monitoring and the ID check introduced by UK because these only protect actual criminals and parents that do not want to do parenting themselves, but let internet do it instead. Such parents should be better trained or fined instead of trying to protect them over the children. Strong encryption for communication tools would guarantee that third parties would not find home addresses of children or their current locations. It would be great to have a requirement for marketplaces to show how long sale for a good has been available or when it started. Having information of when sale is going to end might have adverse effect so I'd rather not require it as it creates Fear Of Missing Out and pushes adults and especially teens and kids to think that they need to spend money fast to avoid missing out on the sale. Also if it's possible to push for education on these matters in member states that would be good as well as these are important matters. Just my two cents.
